item.title: Can Major League Baseball sustain its global momentum?

item.title: Israel calls for Iranian factory evacuation, suggesting attack imminent

item.title: Iran war risks global food shock as fertiliser supplies cut
item.description: Growing number of fertiliser plants forced to shut, threatening rice and other harvests

item.description: Shadow housing minister raised questions over critical partner in housebuilding scheme

item.title: Sovereign AI is a bet on the economies of anti-scale
item.description: Deglobalisation is expensive for individual countries, but a windfall for their suppliers

item.description: Outages mark new phase in government campaign against internet freedom

item.title: Are markets being too complacent about the Iran war?
item.description: Sometimes crises do not blow over as investors might hope

item.title: UK ‘home bias’ drives surge in Isa millionaires, say investment platforms
item.description: UK companies such as Lloyds, Shell and Rolls-Royce featured heavily in Isa portfolios whose value topped £1mn last year

item.title: The Noma affair will leave a very nasty taste in the mouth
item.description: High-end restaurants as a force for moral good was an idea long lacking in self-awareness

item.title: Pinterest’s mood board should include a potential sale
item.description: Many of its users may gather ideas on the platform but shop somewhere else

item.title: AI is dressing up greed as progress on creative rights
item.description: The problem is not that the law is unfit for the 21st century but that it is being flouted

item.description: On Oscars weekend, the famous title’s new chief makes his case

item.description: This year offers something for everyone, but the looming Warner-Paramount deal threatens the whole Hollywood ecosystem

item.description: Emails obtained through a freedom of information request show concerns within the agency about coal files

item.title: Canada supplying 23.6 million oil barrels under IEA release plan
item.description: IEA members agreed this week to release 400 million barrels from strategic reserves to offset supply disruptions from the Middle East war

item.description: The telltale signs of an energy crisis – supply shocks, rations and hoarding – are here

item.description: California court questions billionaire’s expert witness but declines to exclude the testimony from April trial

item.title: Middle East war day 14 as it happened: Washington eases some Russian oil sanctions, Donald Trump says US ‘obliterated’ military assets on Iran’s Kharg Island
item.description: President warns he would attack oil infrastructure if Tehran impeded more shipments through the Strait of Hormuz

item.description: US launches strike on Iran’s Kharg Island supply hub as banks forecast oil could push well above $100

item.description: The non-profit online publication, being developed under the working title Be Giant, is preparing to debut this spring

item.description: Hedging can act as a shock absorber for higher fuel prices

item.description: U.S. government lawsuit accused software maker of hiding hefty termination fees and making it difficult to cancel subscriptions

item.description: But the sugar high may not last

item.description: Jensen Huang to unveil new products at GTC event next week as spending shifts from training to running AI models

item.title: Ottawa to allow more hiring of low-wage temporary foreign workers in rural areas
item.description: Announcement comes during tough period for job seekers, driven by economic uncertainty posed by U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariffs

item.title: UK petrol trade body in dispute with ministers over ‘inflammatory’ comments
item.description: PRA claims staff have been abused by public following government comments suggesting retailers are ‘price gouging’

item.title: Judge blocks subpoenas against U.S. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell
item.description: Evidence suggests Justice Department probe was meant to pressure Powell to lower rates or resign, federal judge says

item.description: Expected cancellation of lucrative races highlights widening economic impact of the Middle East conflict

item.title: Odey told receptionist he was in ‘deep trouble’ after she alleged he tried to kiss her
item.description: Financier had already received a final written warning from his hedge fund about his contact with female staff, court heard

item.description: For an Iranian regime facing existential struggle, pushing crude up is key measure of success

item.description: In the President’s first term, Iran demonstrated what tactics it would use in a confrontation with the U.S. Yet the Administration seems to have no game plan.
